description_long: This is a cylindrical, cross-shaped fire hydrant painted in a bright red color, with visible patches of rust and wear, indicating exposure to outdoor elements. The hydrant features three prominent horizontal outlets and a central vertical body, with a flanged base for secure mounting to the ground. The surface is marked by several white and brownish spots, likely representing chipped paint and corrosion. At the top, there is a small, hexagonal operating nut or cap, which is typically used to open the hydrant. The outlets are capped, but there are no visible chains attaching the caps to the hydrant. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the surface, and the hydrant is not shown in proximity to any curb or sidewalk.
description: A weathered, red fire hydrant with three horizontal outlets, rust patches, and a central operating nut on top. The surface shows signs of wear and corrosion.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cross-shaped body, three horizontal outlets, and the top operating nut, with visible rust and paint wear.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical vertical body and one horizontal outlet, with rust and chipped paint details.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite side of the cross, with all three outlets visible and more pronounced corrosion spots.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the cylindrical body and one outlet, with surface wear and rust.
